    {
      "block_id": "p20_b21",
      "translation": "Although new business value of USD 2.765 billion in 2020 decreased 33% versus 2019, we rapidly adopted new digital tools and relaxation of movement restrictions supported sales recovery."
    },
    {
      "block_id": "p20_b22",
      "translation": "AIA's China business contributed the largest share to Group new business value in 2020 and was the first reporting segment showing sales recovery as the region progressively relaxed lockdowns from March 2020. Excluding Mainland China visitor sales to Hong Kong, which remained low, other segments showed strong recovery in H2, growing 23% versus H1."
    },
    {
      "block_id": "p20_b23",
      "translation": "Group new business momentum remained strong in 2021, with new business value in the first two months 15% higher than the corresponding 2020 period."
    },
    {
      "block_id": "p20_b24",
      "translation": "AIA's China business achieved strong momentum in 2021, with first two months showing very strong growth versus 2020. New business value of USD 968 million in 2020 decreased 17% versus 2019, primarily due to limited sales during initial COVID-19 outbreak in Q1. As restrictions relaxed, momentum improved rapidly, resuming customary seasonal patterns with greater concentration in H1."
    },
    {
      "block_id": "p20_b25",
      "translation": "In 2020, AIA's China business contributed the largest share to Group new business value."
    },
    {
      "block_id": "p20_b26",
      "translation": "Following travel restrictions, Mainland China visitor sales to AIA's Hong Kong business were effectively suspended from early February 2020, causing significant decline. Profit margin declined reflecting underwriting expense overruns from reduced volume, product mix changes and lower interest rates. Macau resumed individual visit scheme end-September 2020, supporting recovery of Mainland China visitor sales, which exceeded one-third of AIA Macau's annualized new premiums in Q4."
    }
  ]
}
